title: Try an Example for the dateFacet Type

sections.heading: Step 1: Retrieve your Service Account access token for the project.
sections.body:
Use your service account to generate an access token, which
authenticates your requests to the Atlas Administration API.
To learn how to generate an access token, see
[Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

sections.heading: Step 2: Make an  call.
Step 1: Create a  configuration file and specify the  type.
Step 2: Connect to the Atlas CLI
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your cluster from the
Atlas CLI. To learn more, see
[Connect from the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli).

sections.heading: Step 3: Create a  index.
Step 1: Connect to your cluster using .
sections.body:
Open [Compass](https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/compass) and connect to your cluster. For
detailed instructions, see [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass).

sections.heading: Step 2: Specify the database and collection.
sections.body: On the Database screen, click the name of the database, then click the name of the collection.

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the  index.
Step 1: Connect to the deployment by using .
sections.body:
In your terminal, connect to your Atlas cloud-hosted
deployment or local deployment from [mongosh](mongodb-shell). For detailed
instructions on how to connect, see
[Connect to a Deployment](mongodb-shell/connect).

sections.heading: Step 2: Switch to the database that contains the collection for which you want to create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**

```shell
 use <database>
```

**Output:**

```shell
switched to db <database>
```

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the  method to create the index.
Step 1: Set up the C project.
sections.body:
In your terminal, navigate to where you want to create your application,
then run the following command to create a directory called
`atlas-search-project` for this project:

```shell
# Create a new directory for the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
```

Add the C driver to your project by following the instructions in the
[MongoDB C Driver documentation](languages/c/c-driver/current/get-started).

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory for the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Set up a CMake application
sections.body:
To configure your application, create a `CMakeLists.txt` file in
your project directory. Then, add the following code to the file:

```txt
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.11)
project(atlas-search-index LANGUAGES C)
add_executable (index.out create_index.c)
find_package(mongoc <version> REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(index.out mongoc::mongoc)

```

The preceding code performs the following actions:

- Configures a C project.

- Creates a `index.out` executable for your application.

- Finds and requires the C driver. Replace the `<version>`
  placeholder with your C driver version number, such as `2.0.0`.

- Links the program to the `libmongoc` library.

> **Note:** In the sample `CMakeLists.txt` file, the `mongoc::mongoc` target
> points to either the static library or the shared library.
> The library type depends on which one is available and
> whichever type the user specifies in the `MONGOC_DEFAULT_IMPORTED_LIBRARY_TYPE`
> CMake configuration setting. If you don't set this value and
> both library types are available, `mongoc::mongoc` uses
> the static library.
>
> You can use the `mongoc::static` target to explicitly use the
> static library or the `mongoc::shared` target to use the shared
> library.

codeBlocks.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.11)
project(atlas-search-index LANGUAGES C)
add_executable (index.out create_index.c)
find_package(mongoc <version> REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(index.out mongoc::mongoc)

In your terminal, navigate to where you want to create your application,

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
Step 1: Set up and initialize the .NET/C# project.
sections.body:
In your terminal, navigate to where you want to create your application,
then run the following command to create a directory called
`atlas-search-project` and initialize your project in that directory:

```shell
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
dotnet new console

# Add the MongoDB .NET/C# Driver to your project
dotnet add package MongoDB.Driver
```

For more detailed installation instructions, see the
[MongoDB C# Driver documentation](csharp/get-started).

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
dotnet new console

# Add the MongoDB .NET/C# Driver to your project
dotnet add package MongoDB.Driver

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
Step 1: Set up and initialize the Go module.
sections.body:
In your terminal, navigate to where you want to create your application,
then run the following command to create a directory called
`atlas-search-project` and initialize your project in that directory:

```shell
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
go mod init atlas-search-project

# Add the MongoDB Go Driver to your project
go get go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/v2/mongo
```

For more detailed installation instructions, see the
[MongoDB Go Driver documentation](go/get-started).

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
go mod init atlas-search-project

# Add the MongoDB Go Driver to your project
go get go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver/v2/mongo

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
Step 1: Set up your Java project with the MongoDB Java driver.
sections.body:
In your IDE, create a new [Maven](https://maven.apache.org/) or
[Gradle](https://gradle.org/) project. Add the Bill of
Materials (BOM) for MongoDB JVM artifacts to your project to
organize dependency versions. The BOM simplifies dependency
management by ensuring that you maintain consistent and compatible
versions of dependencies, such as between the Java driver and
the core driver library. Use the BOM to avoid version conflicts
and simplify upgrades.Select from the following **Maven** and **Gradle** tabs
to view instructions for adding the BOM for each dependency manager:MavenAdd the following code to the `dependencyManagement` list in your
`pom.xml` file:```xml
<dependencyManagement>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mongodb</groupId>
            <artifactId>mongodb-driver-bom</artifactId>
            <version>5.5.1</version>
            <type>pom</type>
            <scope>import</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
</dependencyManagement>
```GradleAdd the following code to dependencies list in your
`build.gradle` file:```groovy
dependencies {
    implementation(platform("org.mongodb:mongodb-driver-bom:5.5.1"))
}
```To view a list of dependencies that the BOM manages, see
the [mongodb-driver-bom dependency listing](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.mongodb/mongodb-driver-bom/5.51)
on the Maven Repository website.After adding the BOM, select from the following **Maven** and **Gradle** tabs to view
instructions for adding the MongoDB Java Sync Driver as a dependency
in your project:MavenAdd the following code to the `dependencies` array in your Maven
project's `pom.xml` file:```xml
<dependencies>
    <dependency>    
      <groupId>org.mongodb</groupId>    
      <artifactId>mongodb-driver-sync</artifactId>    
      <version>4.11.1</version>    
    </dependency>
</dependencies>

```GradleAdd the following to the `dependencies` array in your Gradle
project's `build.gradle` file:```json
dependencies {
   // MongoDB Java Sync Driver v4.11.0 or later
   implementation 'org.mongodb:mongodb-driver-sync'
}

```Run your package manager to install the dependencies to your project.For more detailed installation instructions and version compatibility, see
the [MongoDB Java Driver documentation](java/sync/current/get-started).

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Compile and run the file to create the index.
Step 1: Initialize your Node.js project.
sections.body:
```shell
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
npm init -y

# Add the MongoDB Node.js Driver to your project
npm install mongodb
```

For detailed installation instructions, see the
[MongoDB Node Driver documentation](node).

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory and initialize the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project
npm init -y

# Add the MongoDB Node.js Driver to your project
npm install mongodb

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
Step 1: Install the MongoDB Python Driver.
sections.body:
```shell
# Create a new directory for the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project

# Install PyMongo
pip install pymongo
```

For detailed installation instructions, see
[MongoDB Python Driver documentation](languages/python/pymongo-driver/get-started).

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory for the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project

# Install PyMongo
pip install pymongo

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
sections.body:
Paste the following code into a file named `create_index.py`.```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["<database>"]
collection = database["<collection>"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": True|False,
            "fields": {
                "<field_name>":{
                   "type": "string",
                   "analyzer": "<analyzer-name>",
                   "searchAnalyzer": "<search-analyzer-name>",
                   "indexOptions": "docs|freqs|positions|offsets",
                   "store": True|False,
                   "ignoreAbove": <integer>,
                   "similarity": { "type": "bm25|boolean|stableTfl" },
                   "multi": { <string-field-definition> },
                   "norms": "include|omit"
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```Replace `<connection-string>` with the connection string for your
Atlas cluster or local Atlas deployment.Atlas ClusterYour connection string should use the following format:```
mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@<clusterName>.<hostname>.mongodb.net
```To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).Local or Self-ManagedYour connection string should use the
following format:```
mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
```To learn more, see [Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).

codeBlocks.content:
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["<database>"]
collection = database["<collection>"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": True|False,
            "fields": {
                "<field_name>":{
                   "type": "string",
                   "analyzer": "<analyzer-name>",
                   "searchAnalyzer": "<search-analyzer-name>",
                   "indexOptions": "docs|freqs|positions|offsets",
                   "store": True|False,
                   "ignoreAbove": <integer>,
                   "similarity": { "type": "bm25|boolean|stableTfl" },
                   "multi": { <string-field-definition> },
                   "norms": "include|omit"
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**```shell
python create_index.py
```**Output:**```
New index name: default
```The MongoDB Search `string` type takes the following parameters:| Option          | Type                    | Necessity | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Default |
| --------------- | ----------------------- | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| type            | string                  | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this field type. Value must be string.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |         |
| analyzer        | string                  | Optional  | Name of a built-in or custom analyzer to use for indexing the field. If you don't specify a value, inherits an analyzer by default in the following order:The analyzer option for the index if specified.The lucene.standard analyzer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |         |
| searchAnalyzer  | string                  | Optional  | Analyzer to use when querying the field. If you don't specify a value, inherits an analyzer by default in the following order:The analyzer option for this field if specified.The searchAnalyzer option for the index if specified.The analyzer option for the index if specified.The lucene.standard analyzer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |         |
| indexOptions    | string                  | Optional  | Amount of information to store for the indexed field. Value can be one of the following:docs - Only indexes documents. The frequency and position of the indexed term are ignored. Only a single occurence of the term is reflected in the score.freqs - Only indexes documents and term frequency. The position of the indexed term is ignored.positions - Indexes documents, term frequency, and term positions.offsets - (Default) Indexes documents, term frequency, term positions, and term offsets. This option is required for highlight.                                                                                                                                              | offsets |
| store           | boolean                 | Optional  | Flag that indicates whether to store the exact document text as well as the analyzed values in the index. Value can be true or false. The value for this option must be true for highlight.To reduce the index size and performance footprint, we recommend setting store to false. To learn more, see MongoDB Search Index Performance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | true    |
| ignoreAbove     | int                     | Optional  | Maximum number of characters in the value of the field to index. MongoDB Search doesn't index if the field value is greater than the specified number of characters.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |         |
| similarity.type | String                  | Optional  | Name of the similarity algorithm to use with this string mapping when scoring with the text, phrase, or queryString operators. Value can be one of the following: bm25, boolean, or stableTfl.To learn more about the available similarity algorithms, see Score Details.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | bm25    |
| multi           | String Field Definition | Optional  | String field to index with the name of the alternate analyzer specified in the multi object. To learn more about specifying the multi object, see Multi Analyzer and an example below.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |         |
| norms           | string                  | Optional  | String that specifies whether to include or omit the field length in the result when scoring. The length of the field is determined by the number of tokens produced by the analyzer for the field. Value can be one of the following:include - to include the field length when scoring.omit - to omit the field length when scoring.If value is include, MongoDB Search uses the length of the field to determine the higher score when scoring. For example, if two documents match a MongoDB Search query, the document with the shorter field length scores higher than the document with the longer field length.If value is omit, MongoDB Search ignores the field length when scoring. | include |The following index definition examples use collections in the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets). After you
[Load Sample Data Into Atlas](load-sample-data), you can use
these examples to index fields as the dateFacet type.To learn how to set up and run the following example code, see
Define the Index for the token Type.Basic ExampleThe following index definition for the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection in the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) indexes the
`title` field with string values.```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["movies"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": False,
            "fields": {
                "title": {
                   "type": "string"
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```Multi ExampleThe following index definition for the `sample_mflix.movies`
collection in the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets)
indexes the `title` field with string values. It also specifies the
analyzers [Language awareness](ref-language-analyzers) and
[Language awareness](ref-language-analyzers) as alternate analyzers for
the `title` field, and the `stableTfl` similarity algorithm as an
alternate similarity algorithm.```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["movies"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": False,
            "fields": {
                "title": {
                   "type": "string",
                   "multi": {
                       "english": {
                           "type": "string",
                           "analyzer": "lucene.english"
                       },
                       "french": {
                           "type": "string",
                           "analyzer": "lucene.french"
                       }, 
                       "stableSimilarity": {
                           "type": "string",
                           "similarity": { "type": "stableTfl" }
                       }
                   }
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
Step 3: Create the index.
Step 1: Install the MongoDB Python Driver.
sections.body:
```shell
# Create a new directory for the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project

# Install PyMongo
pip install pymongo
```

For detailed installation instructions, see
[MongoDB Python Driver documentation](languages/python/pymongo-driver/get-started).

codeBlocks.content:
# Create a new directory for the project
mkdir atlas-search-project && cd atlas-search-project

# Install PyMongo
pip install pymongo

sections.heading: Step 2: Define the index.
sections.body:
Paste the following code into a file named `create_index.py`.```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["<database>"]
collection = database["<collection>"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": True|False,
            "fields": {
                "<field_name>":{
                   "type": "string",
                   "analyzer": "<analyzer-name>",
                   "searchAnalyzer": "<search-analyzer-name>",
                   "indexOptions": "docs|freqs|positions|offsets",
                   "store": True|False,
                   "ignoreAbove": <integer>,
                   "similarity": { "type": "bm25|boolean|stableTfl" },
                   "multi": { <string-field-definition> },
                   "norms": "include|omit"
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```Replace `<connection-string>` with the connection string for your
Atlas cluster or local Atlas deployment.Atlas ClusterYour connection string should use the following format:```
mongodb+srv://<db_username>:<db_password>@<clusterName>.<hostname>.mongodb.net
```To learn more, see [Driver](connect-via-driver).Local or Self-ManagedYour connection string should use the
following format:```
mongodb://localhost:<port-number>/?directConnection=true
```To learn more, see [Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).

from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"

    name="default",

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")

sections.heading: Step 3: Create the index.
sections.body:
**Input:**```shell
python create_index.py
```**Output:**```
New index name: default
```The MongoDB Search `string` type takes the following parameters:| Option          | Type                    | Necessity | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Default |
| --------------- | ----------------------- | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| type            | string                  | Required  | Human-readable label that identifies this field type. Value must be string.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |         |
| analyzer        | string                  | Optional  | Name of a built-in or custom analyzer to use for indexing the field. If you don't specify a value, inherits an analyzer by default in the following order:The analyzer option for the index if specified.The lucene.standard analyzer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |         |
| searchAnalyzer  | string                  | Optional  | Analyzer to use when querying the field. If you don't specify a value, inherits an analyzer by default in the following order:The analyzer option for this field if specified.The searchAnalyzer option for the index if specified.The analyzer option for the index if specified.The lucene.standard analyzer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |         |
| indexOptions    | string                  | Optional  | Amount of information to store for the indexed field. Value can be one of the following:docs - Only indexes documents. The frequency and position of the indexed term are ignored. Only a single occurence of the term is reflected in the score.freqs - Only indexes documents and term frequency. The position of the indexed term is ignored.positions - Indexes documents, term frequency, and term positions.offsets - (Default) Indexes documents, term frequency, term positions, and term offsets. This option is required for highlight.                                                                                                                                              | offsets |
| store           | boolean                 | Optional  | Flag that indicates whether to store the exact document text as well as the analyzed values in the index. Value can be true or false. The value for this option must be true for highlight.To reduce the index size and performance footprint, we recommend setting store to false. To learn more, see MongoDB Search Index Performance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | true    |
| ignoreAbove     | int                     | Optional  | Maximum number of characters in the value of the field to index. MongoDB Search doesn't index if the field value is greater than the specified number of characters.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |         |
| similarity.type | String                  | Optional  | Name of the similarity algorithm to use with this string mapping when scoring with the text, phrase, or queryString operators. Value can be one of the following: bm25, boolean, or stableTfl.To learn more about the available similarity algorithms, see Score Details.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | bm25    |
| multi           | String Field Definition | Optional  | String field to index with the name of the alternate analyzer specified in the multi object. To learn more about specifying the multi object, see Multi Analyzer and an example below.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |         |
| norms           | string                  | Optional  | String that specifies whether to include or omit the field length in the result when scoring. The length of the field is determined by the number of tokens produced by the analyzer for the field. Value can be one of the following:include - to include the field length when scoring.omit - to omit the field length when scoring.If value is include, MongoDB Search uses the length of the field to determine the higher score when scoring. For example, if two documents match a MongoDB Search query, the document with the shorter field length scores higher than the document with the longer field length.If value is omit, MongoDB Search ignores the field length when scoring. | include |The following index definition examples use collections in the
[sample data sets](available-sample-datasets). After you
[Load Sample Data Into Atlas](load-sample-data), you can use
these examples to index fields as the dateFacet type.To learn how to set up and run the following example code, see
Define the Index for the token Type.Basic ExampleThe following index definition for the
`sample_mflix.movies` collection in the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets) indexes the
`title` field with string values.```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["movies"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": False,
            "fields": {
                "title": {
                   "type": "string"
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```Multi ExampleThe following index definition for the `sample_mflix.movies`
collection in the [sample data sets](available-sample-datasets)
indexes the `title` field with string values. It also specifies the
analyzers [Language awareness](ref-language-analyzers) and
[Language awareness](ref-language-analyzers) as alternate analyzers for
the `title` field, and the `stableTfl` similarity algorithm as an
alternate similarity algorithm.```python
from pymongo import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel

# connect to your Atlas deployment
uri = "<connection-string>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# set namespace
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["movies"]

# define your MongoDB Search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
    definition={
        "mappings": {
            "dynamic": False,
            "fields": {
                "title": {
                   "type": "string",
                   "multi": {
                       "english": {
                           "type": "string",
                           "analyzer": "lucene.english"
                       },
                       "french": {
                           "type": "string",
                           "analyzer": "lucene.french"
                       }, 
                       "stableSimilarity": {
                           "type": "string",
                           "similarity": { "type": "stableTfl" }
                       }
                   }
                }
            }
        },
    },
    name="default",
)

# create the index
result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print(f"New index name: {result}")
```
